trustSimpliers.description: Your account and your followers are always safe. Every automation uses a Meta-approved app. The automation only shares general, guidance-focused content and never gives definite predictions or health and finance advice. Contact information is stored encrypted, and no sensitive details are requested in DMs.

preFooter.title: Share, inspire and grow from your {highlight}
preFooter.description: Free horoscope readings, ritual guides and worksheets go out automatically the moment a follower reaches you, and pricing and booking questions never go unanswered.

qas.question: Does the automation give my followers a personal reading or prediction?
qas.answer: No. It only shares general content, informs people and suggests you or a session. You do the reading yourself, and it never gives definite predictions or health and finance advice.

qas.question: Is it suitable for life coaching and astrology, and does it give medical or psychological advice?
qas.answer: No. Life coaching is not therapy and astrology is not medical or scientific advice. The automation only shares content and guides people to you, it never gives psychological or medical advice.

qas.question: Does it work with Calendly or my payment system?
qas.answer: Yes. You can share Calendly, your own booking system or payment links like iyzico and Stripe right in the DM.

qas.question: Can I send my free horoscope reading or guide automatically from a comment?
qas.answer: Yes. Anyone who comments your keyword gets your reading, guide or worksheet by DM automatically.

qas.question: Will my posts reach the explore page?
qas.answer: Fast engagement triggers the algorithm and the post lands on explore. The organic followers come from an audience already interested in the topic.

qas.question: Is data safe and compliant?
qas.answer: The automation is Meta-approved and encrypted. No sensitive details are requested in DMs, only contact information and general interest are collected.

qas.question: Can I start for free?
qas.answer: Yes. The free plan covers up to 500 active conversations per month. You can upgrade anytime as your audience grows.

privacyPolicy.title: Simpliers Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.text: Effective Date: 13/10/2025 {br}Last Updated: 13/10/2025{br}{br}{simpliers} ("Company", "we", "our"), commits to protecting the privacy and security of our users' personal data. This Privacy Policy has been prepared in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698 (KVKK) and the European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).{br}{br}This policy applies to all personal data obtained through {simpliers} website, mobile applications, social media automation tools, form services and other digital platforms.

content.title: What Data Do We Collect?
content.text: Identity Information: Name, surname, username.{br}Contact Information: Email address, phone number.{br}Account Information: Social media account connections, access permissions granted through Meta API.{br}Payment & Billing Information: Card information (only through payment provider), billing information.{br}Technical Information: IP address, device type, browser information, operating system, log records.{br}Usage Data: In-platform activities, created forms, campaign statistics.{br}Content Data: Social media posts, comments, likes, DM contents (managed through Meta API).{br}Form Data: Name, email, phone, survey responses etc. collected by users through {simpliers} forms.{br}Cookie Data: Data collected through cookies and similar technologies regarding site usage.

content.title: For What Purposes Do We Process Data?
content.text: Social Media Automation: Performing operations such as posting, commenting, liking, sending DMs on Instagram on behalf of users through Meta's official APIs.{br}Service Provision: Account management, user experience improvement, integration provision.{br}Customer Communication: Responding to support requests, announcements and notifications.{br}Legal Obligations: Tax, billing and reporting to official authorities.{br}Development & Analysis: Improving our products, analyzing user behavior.{br}Security: Preventing spam, fake accounts, abuse and fraud.{br}Marketing (with permission): Email newsletters, promotional messages, targeted ads.

content.title: Meta API Usage
content.text: {simpliers} provides social media automation services only through Meta's official APIs.{br}User data is processed only upon user instruction.{br}Authorization tokens provided by Meta are stored securely and not shared with third parties.{br}All operations comply with Meta Platform Policies.{br}User data is never processed or sold without permission.

content.title: Data Sharing
content.text: Service Providers: Hosting, storage, analytics, email sending, payment processing.{br}Business Partners: Only with explicit consent.{br}Official Authorities: In accordance with legal obligations.{br}Corporate Transactions: In case of merger, acquisition or sale.

content.title: Data Retention Period
content.text: Giveaway Data: At least 15 days.{br}Form Responses: Until user deletes the form.{br}Meta API Permissions: Until user revokes access or deletes account.{br}Invoice and Payment Records: At least 10 years as required by tax legislation.{br}Cookie Data: Maximum 2 years.

content.title: International Data Transfer
content.text: Some service providers may be located abroad (e.g. Europe, USA).{br}Standard Contract Clauses are applied under GDPR.{br}Board-approved security measures are taken under KVKK.

content.title: User Rights
content.text: Requesting access to personal data.{br}Requesting correction of incorrect or incomplete data.{br}Requesting deletion of data.{br}Requesting restriction of processing.{br}Objecting to processing activities.{br}Requesting transfer of data to another provider.{br}Withdrawing consent.{br}Filing complaints to KVKK Board in Turkey or relevant authority in EU.

content.title: Age Restriction
content.text: {simpliers} services cannot be used by persons under 13 years of age.{br}Users aged 13-18 can only use the service with the consent of their parents or legal guardians.{br}If data belonging to a person under 13 is detected, it will be deleted immediately.{br}Parents can request deletion of their children's data by writing to [email protected].{br}Under KVKK, legal representative consent may be required for those under 18 in Turkey.{br}Under GDPR, the minimum age limit in EU countries is between 13-16 and {simpliers} complies with this.

content.title: Security Measures
content.text: Data received through Meta API is encrypted.{br}SSL/TLS security is implemented.{br}Regular security tests are conducted.{br}Only authorized personnel can access data.

content.title: Policy Changes
content.text: Privacy policy may be updated from time to time.{br}Significant changes will be announced via email or in-platform notification.

qas.question: What can {simpliers} {chat} do for creators on Instagram?
qas.answer: Automate DMs, comments, replies, link sending and content requests. All while keeping your inbox organized so you can stay focused on creating and growing your Instagram account.

qas.answer: Yes. You stay in control at every step. Automations simply help you reply faster and handle repetitive messages while you focus on creating on Instagram.

qas.answer: Not at all. Everything works visually. You can set up automations in minutes without writing a single line of code.

qas.answer: Yes, 100%. {simpliers} is approved by Meta and only uses official APIs. Your account is always protected.

qas.answer: No. Replies are personalized and feel natural. You can customize the tone, wording, and timing so everything sounds like you.

qas.answer: Yes. Set a keyword like 'LINK' or 'INFO' and anyone who comments it gets a DM with your link automatically.

qas.answer: Yes. Automations work on Reels comments just like regular posts. More replies mean more engagement, and that pushes your Reels to Explore.

qas.answer: Yes. The free plan lets you engage with up to 500 people. Upgrade anytime when you're ready for more.

howTo.title: How to use the UTM link
howTo.description: Four steps to a measurable campaign. If you want to plug it into a Comment-to-DM automation, the last step takes you straight to the use case.

steps.title: Paste your destination link
steps.description: Drop the URL you want to send traffic to into the Link field above. Any existing UTM parameters are stripped out automatically and split into the fields below.

steps.title: Fill in the campaign fields

explainer.title: What is a UTM link?
explainer.body1: A UTM link is just a regular URL with small tracking tags glued on. Analytics tools like Google Analytics, Plausible or Mixpanel read those tags and group the traffic. So you see exactly where each visitor came from.
explainer.body2: Especially powerful for Instagram. Tag your bio link, story stickers, DM automations and post CTAs separately — and stop guessing which channel actually converts.
explainer.param1: utm_source — the platform the traffic comes from.
explainer.param2: utm_medium — the channel type: social, email, cpc, and so on.
explainer.param4: utm_term — usually a paid ad keyword.
explainer.param5: utm_content — for A/B testing different links inside the same campaign.

items.question: How do I use a UTM link inside a Comment-to-DM automation?
items.answer: Drop the UTM link you built here into the reply message of your Simpliers CHAT Comment-to-DM automation. Tag it as utm_source=instagram, utm_medium=dm, utm_campaign=your-automation-name. In Google Analytics or Plausible you'll see clicks from that automation as a separate source.

items.question: What's the best UTM combo for Instagram?
items.answer: Standard formula: utm_source=instagram, utm_medium=bio/dm/story/reels (depending on where the link lives), utm_campaign=campaign-or-automation-name. If the same automation runs on several posts, separate them with utm_content (e.g. story-cta, reel-pin).

items.question: Should every trigger post of the same automation use a different UTM?
items.answer: Yes. Keep the same campaign name but change utm_content for each trigger post. You'll see exactly which post, reel or story brings the most DMs and clicks. Basically a built-in A/B test.

items.question: Are UTM tags case-sensitive?
items.answer: Yes. Most analytics tools treat 'Instagram' and 'instagram' as two different sources. Pick one convention (lowercase is safest) and stick with it across all your campaigns.

items.question: Do UTM parameters affect SEO?
items.answer: No. Search engines ignore UTM parameters for ranking; they're only used by analytics tools. Just avoid tagging links inside your own site that can break your attribution.

items.question: Should I build separate links for bio, story sticker and DM automation?
items.answer: Yes. Tag every touchpoint with its own utm_medium: medium=bio for the bio link, medium=story for story stickers, medium=dm for DM automations. Even if they all point to the same campaign, this is the only way to see which channel is actually doing the work.

items.question: What is the QR code good for?
items.answer: Download the QR for your tagged link and put it on flyers, packaging, slides, in-store materials or a podcast cover. Every scan still carries the same UTM tags into Analytics so you can measure offline traffic too.

tldr.title: What is an Instagram image splitter?
tldr.body: An Instagram image splitter cuts a single photo into evenly sized tiles arranged in a grid (typically 3x3 for a full nine-post feed banner). Upload your photo, choose grid size, and download each tile in posting order. Post the tiles from bottom-right to top-left so the full image appears on your profile.

explainer.title: How Instagram grid posts work
explainer.body1: Instagram displays your latest posts in a three-column feed. By posting tiles of a single image in reverse order (last tile first), you can build a banner, mural, or storytelling sequence that spans your whole profile.
explainer.body2: A 3x3 grid uses 9 posts (one square row across, three deep). Wider grids (3x4, 3x5) let your banner stretch deeper into the feed but require more uploads. Pick a grid size that fits your content calendar.

rows.use: Profile banner, brand reveals, product launches.

howTo.title: How to split an image for Instagram
howTo.description: Four steps to a perfect grid. No signup, no upload to a server.

steps.title: Upload your image
steps.description: Pick a JPG, PNG or WebP under 15 MB. For a clean 3x3, start with a square (1:1) image at least 2160 x 2160 px.

steps.title: Choose your grid size
steps.description: Use a preset or set rows and columns by hand. 3x3 is the classic Instagram grid. Wider grids work great for banners and storytelling.

steps.title: Preview the tiles
steps.description: Each tile shows its posting number. Tile 1 is the top-left of the final grid on your profile.

steps.title: Download and post in reverse order
steps.description: Save every tile, then upload them to Instagram from the highest-numbered tile to the lowest. The grid then displays correctly on your profile.

items.question: What is the best image size for a 3x3 Instagram grid?
items.answer: Start with a square image of at least 2160 x 2160 pixels. After splitting into 9 tiles, each one will be 720 x 720 px, which is the recommended minimum for Instagram posts.

items.question: In which order should I post the tiles?
items.answer: Instagram shows your newest post first, in the top-left of your profile grid. To make the full image appear correctly, post your tiles in reverse order: bottom-right tile first, top-left tile last.

items.question: Does this tool upload my image to a server?
items.answer: No. The splitter runs entirely in your browser using the HTML5 Canvas API. Your image never leaves your device.

items.question: What grid sizes can I create?
items.answer: Any combination from 2x2 up to 5x5. The classic Instagram grid is 3x3 (9 tiles), but 3x1 banners, 3x2 promos, and longer 3x4 or 3x5 murals are popular too.

items.question: Can I split a portrait or landscape photo?
items.answer: Yes. The tool divides whatever you upload into equally sized tiles based on the rows and columns you pick. For a feed banner, a square source image gives the cleanest result.

items.question: Will the tiles look pixelated?
items.answer: Quality depends on your source image. As a rule of thumb, multiply your target tile size (720 px) by rows and columns to get the minimum source resolution. A 3x3 grid needs at least 2160 x 2160 px.

items.question: What file formats are supported?
items.answer: JPG, PNG and WebP up to 15 MB. The tool outputs PNG tiles to preserve quality.

items.question: How does this connect to Instagram DM automation?
items.answer: Grid posts drive comments. Pair the splitter with a Simpliers CHAT Comment-to-DM automation so every comment on your new banner triggers a personalized DM with your link, discount code or freebie.

explainer.title: What is Instagram DM ROI?
explainer.body1: Instagram DM ROI is the return you get on the time and money you spend talking to followers in DMs. Manual replies cost hours every week. Automation replaces those hours with instant answers and turns DMs into a real revenue channel.
explainer.body2: Instagram DMs open at around 90 percent, far above email. Automating them means faster replies, more conversions and fewer leads lost in the inbox.

explainer.param2: Extra revenue from faster response times and higher conversion rates.
explainer.param3: Monthly tool cost compared to a virtual assistant or your own hourly rate.

howTo.title: How to calculate your DM automation ROI
howTo.description: Four steps to a realistic number you can trust before you commit.

steps.title: Collect your weekly DM volume
steps.description: Open Instagram Insights or your inbox and count an average week. Include comments that turn into DM conversations.

steps.title: Estimate the time per manual reply
steps.description: Time yourself for a few real replies and take the average. Include reading, writing and follow-ups.

steps.title: Plug your numbers into the calculator
steps.description: Enter weekly DMs, minutes per reply, your hourly rate, average sale value and the tool cost. Pick the response time that matches reality today.

steps.title: Read the verdict and act
steps.description: Look at net ROI and payback period. If the math works, set up your first comment-to-DM automation on Simpliers CHAT and start measuring real results.

items.question: Is Instagram DM automation actually worth it for small accounts?
items.answer: Yes, when you get even 50 DM requests a week and a clear offer. The calculator usually shows positive ROI from the first month because the tool replaces hours of manual replying and recovers leads that would otherwise go cold.

items.question: Is DM automation safe for my Instagram account?
items.answer: Yes when you use a Meta-approved partner. Simpliers CHAT works through the official Instagram Graph API and follows Meta's policies, so accounts are not at risk.

items.question: Why is response time so important for ROI?
items.answer: Research shows that replies within five minutes convert several times better than replies after an hour. Slow responses lose interest, momentum and trust, so faster replies directly raise conversion rates.

items.question: How does the calculator estimate extra revenue?
items.answer: It takes your weekly DM volume, applies a baseline conversion rate of three percent and multiplies the uplift tied to your current response time by your average sale value. Faster response times produce higher uplift.

items.question: Can I trust the conversion uplift numbers?
items.answer: They are conservative estimates based on industry data. Real results depend on your offer, audience and how good your automation flow is. Treat the output as a serious starting point, then validate with your own data.

items.question: What if my product needs a long sales conversation?
items.answer: Automation still helps. Use it to qualify leads, share resources and book calls automatically, then take over the conversation as a human once the lead is warm.

items.question: Do I need a big follower count for this to make sense?
items.answer: No. ROI depends on DM volume and offer value, not follower count. A small engaged account with a real product often gets higher returns than a large account with no offer.

items.question: How is this different from a chatbot or a virtual assistant?
items.answer: A virtual assistant costs a monthly salary and works in shifts. A chatbot tries to talk like a human. DM automation reacts instantly to comments, story replies and keywords with predictable flows you control.

items.question: How fast can I set up DM automation on Simpliers CHAT?
items.answer: Most users launch their first comment-to-DM automation in under five minutes: connect Instagram, pick a keyword, write the reply and publish.

items.question: What if my net ROI in the calculator is negative?
items.answer: Check your inputs first. If DM volume or sale value is genuinely low, automation may not be the priority. Focus on content and offer until volume grows, then come back to this calculator.

whatIsInstagramDmAutomation.title: What is Instagram DM Automation and How Does It Work?
whatIsInstagramDmAutomation.description: A straightforward guide explaining how Instagram DM automation works, which triggers Meta supports, and what it can do for your account.

whatItIs.title: What it is
whatItIs.p1: Instagram DM automation is a system that automatically sends pre-written messages whenever someone comments on your post, replies to your story, or sends you a direct message containing specific keywords you've set.
whatItIs.p2: Instead of manually typing the same replies hundreds of times, you can save time and automate your engagements using Meta-approved applications.

howItWorks.title: How does DM automation work?
howItWorks.p1: Every Instagram DM automation is built on 4 core building blocks:
howItWorks.step1: Set a Trigger: Choose from comments, story replies, story tags, DMs, or live stream interactions.
howItWorks.step2: Prepare Your Message: Write the text you want to send; you can also add links, images, videos, or voice notes if you like.
howItWorks.step3: Add a Condition (Optional): You can require the user to follow you before the message is sent.
howItWorks.step4: Go Live: Activate the automation and watch Simpliers CHAT do the work for you.

triggers.title: Triggers supported by Meta
triggers.p1: The Meta platform officially supports 5 different triggers tailored for various scenarios:
triggers.commentDesc: Comments left under your shared posts or Reels videos.
triggers.storyDesc: Direct messages received in response to your stories.
triggers.storyTagDesc: Other users mentioning or tagging your account in their own stories.
triggers.dmDesc: Direct messages that contain specific keywords defined by you.
triggers.liveDesc: Real-time comments written by viewers during a live broadcast.

benefits.title: Why is DM automation important?
benefits.p1: Remember: DM automation isn't designed to change your brand's voice, but to provide your users with instant replies.
benefits.b1: 24/7 Uninterrupted Communication: It responds within seconds, even while you sleep.
benefits.b2: Algorithm Friendly: By boosting the comment traffic under your posts, it increases your chances of hitting the Explore page.
benefits.b3: Data Collection: It easily collects valuable contact info like emails, phone numbers, and addresses right through DM.
benefits.b4: Personalization: Instead of copy-paste responses, it delivers custom messages addressing the user by their name.

isItSafe.title: Is my account safe?
isItSafe.p1: Yes, absolutely safe. Simpliers CHAT is a Meta-approved Tech Partner. It utilizes the official Instagram Graph API infrastructure.
isItSafe.botVsApiTitle: The difference between Bots and APIs
isItSafe.botVsApiDesc: Illegal bots mimic human behavior, which can lead to your account getting banned. The official API, however, operates securely through endpoints authorized by Meta.
isItSafe.queueDesc: During high-traffic campaign periods, your messages are automatically queued to comply with Meta's limits. This ensures they are sent at the safest speed without any risk of being flagged as spam.

summary.title: Summary
summary.p1: Instagram DM automation is a system that analyzes comments on your posts and delivers your pre-defined automated direct messages (DMs) to your audience in full compliance with Meta's official guidelines.
summary.k1: Triggers: comments, story replies, story mentions, DMs, live stream comments.
summary.k2: 100% compatible with Instagram Professional (Business and Creator) accounts.
summary.k3: Once the account is connected, setting up an automation with ready-to-use templates takes about 2 minutes.
summary.k4: Use cases range from comment-to-DM flows and lead generation to hiding toxic comments and giveaway automations.

qas.answer: No. Instagram DM automation only works on Business or Creator accounts. Personal accounts are outside the scope of Meta's API.

qas.answer: Simpliers CHAT offers a free plan. You can test all the core features of the system without entering credit card information, and upgrade your plan if needed.

qas.question: Can I manage multiple Instagram accounts?
qas.answer: Yes. You can connect as many Instagram accounts as you like to Simpliers CHAT and set up independent automations for each one.

qas.question: How fast are the DMs sent?
qas.answer: Thanks to our smart queue infrastructure that works in full harmony with Meta APIs, your messages are sent at an optimized, secure speed. Even during peak campaign periods, it automatically queues deliveries to protect your account, ensuring your messages reach your target audience at the highest and most seamless speed possible without any spam risk.

qas.question: Can I set up automation for multiple accounts?
qas.answer: Yes, you can connect as many Instagram Business or Creator accounts as you want and run separate automations on each.

isDmAutomationSafe.title: Is Instagram DM automation safe?
isDmAutomationSafe.description: Yes, Instagram DM automation is completely safe but only if the app you use uses Meta's official Graph API infrastructure. There are safe tools, but there are also risky software that can lead to your account being permanently disabled. In this guide, you will learn how to choose the right tool without putting your account at risk.

toc.officialApi: Why the official API is safe

summary.title: Summary
summary.safe1: Are Meta-approved, use the Graph API infrastructure.
summary.safe2: Never ask for your Instagram password. They work with authorization through Instagram.
summary.safe3: Only work on Business and Content Creator accounts.
summary.risky1: Simulate human clicks in the browser.
summary.risky2: Demand your Instagram password to log in.
summary.risky3: Claim that they also work on personal accounts.
summary.risky4: Carry a high risk of restriction and permanent disabling.

officialApi.title: Why is the official Meta Graph API 100% safe?
officialApi.p1: Meta built the automation system itself so that businesses and creators can respond to messages at scale. The 3 main reasons it is safe are:
officialApi.metaApprovedDesc: Only platforms that pass Meta's strict reviews can use these APIs.
officialApi.policyBakedDesc: The messaging limits and rules set by Meta are applied automatically; they prevent you from making mistakes.
officialApi.noPasswordDesc: You connect via Meta Login (OAuth). The application never sees or stores your Instagram password.

dangerousTools.title: "Red flags" that lead to account restriction
dangerousTools.p1: If an automation tool does even one of the following, there is a major security risk there.
dangerousTools.passwordTitle: If it asks for your Instagram password
dangerousTools.passwordDesc: Any tool that logs in from another location on your behalf gets caught by Meta's radar and the account is restricted.
dangerousTools.botTitle: If it runs a browser/app in the background
dangerousTools.botDesc: Click simulators that mimic humans with robotic timings are instantly detected by Meta's advanced algorithms.
dangerousTools.scrapeDesc: Tools that try to access follower, comment or DM data in the background without your permission violate developer policies.
dangerousTools.massDmTitle: If it promises "mass DM to followers"
dangerousTools.massDmDesc: Meta only allows automated DMs to be sent to users who initiated the interaction themselves (commenting, replying to a story, etc.). Sending mass DMs to your followers without them interacting with you is a cause for restriction.

verifyChecklist.title: 5-step safety verification
verifyChecklist.p1: Before connecting your account to a tool, run through this quick checklist:
verifyChecklist.c1: When logging in, does a Meta/Instagram login window open?
verifyChecklist.c2: Does the application have a verifiable Meta Business / Technology Partner badge?
verifyChecklist.c3: Does it require you to use a Business or Creator account?
verifyChecklist.c4: Are the features it promises compatible with Meta's official triggers (comment, story reply, story mention, DM, live)?
verifyChecklist.c5: Are there independent references from long-term users of this tool?

whoShouldCare.title: Who needs to pay more attention?
whoShouldCare.p1: Accounts with 5,000+ followers & brands: If your account is a revenue channel for you, getting a 2-week restriction because of the wrong tool choice means direct financial loss. As your audience grows, your risk tolerance should decrease.
whoShouldCare.p2: Small-scale accounts: Although restrictions are not fatal for accounts under 1,000 followers, when setting up the official API is just as easy as setting up illegal bots, there is no need to seek adventure.

qas.question: Will Instagram disable my account if I use DM automation?
qas.answer: No. If you use Meta-approved technology partner apps like Simpliers CHAT, your account is at no risk. Your messages go through Meta's own approved channels.

qas.question: Are old-school Instagram bots still risky in 2026?
qas.answer: Yes, more than ever. Meta's detection systems improve every year. Browser-based bots get shadowbanned or have accounts disabled faster today than they did three years ago.

qas.question: Can I mass-DM people who have never interacted with me?
qas.answer: No. No safe and compliant tool does this. To prevent spam, Meta only allows replies to interactions initiated by the user (comment, DM, story reply).

qas.question: I accidentally used a risky/password-asking tool, what should I do?
qas.answer: Immediately disconnect that tool, change your Instagram password, go to Facebook Business Settings and revoke the app's access permissions, and switch to a Meta-approved official partner without losing any time.

collectEmails.title: How to collect emails from Instagram
collectEmails.description: Your followers are rented attention — your email list is owned. This guide shows how to turn Instagram comments into email subscribers with Simpliers CHAT's Data Collection node, no external landing page required.

summary.title: Summary
summary.p1: An email list is the only audience asset you fully own. With Simpliers CHAT you gate your lead magnet behind one DM question and collect verified emails straight from the conversation. No landing page.
summary.k1: Use a CTA keyword (CHECKLIST, TEMPLATE) to start the flow from a comment.
summary.k2: Drop in the Data Collection node with reply type Email. Validation is automatic.
summary.k3: Deliver the lead magnet only after a valid email is captured.
summary.k4: Push emails to Mailchimp, ConvertKit or any ESP via External Request.

whyEmail.title: Why email beats followers
whyEmail.p1: Followers are rented attention. Meta decides who sees your posts and when. One algorithm change can cut your reach overnight.
whyEmail.p2: Emails are different. You own them. Open rates on a clean creator list still beat Instagram's organic reach by a wide margin.
whyEmail.p3: Creators with 5K-100K followers hit a wall here: big audience, tiny conversion. The fix is to turn rented attention into owned subscribers.

howItWorks.title: The email collection flow
howItWorks.p1: Instagram DMs are the capture form. No external landing page. Four steps:
howItWorks.step1: Post or Reel with a clear lead magnet promise and a CTA keyword.
howItWorks.step2: Simpliers CHAT detects the comment and sends a friendly DM.
howItWorks.step3: Data Collection node asks for an email. Invalid formats retry automatically.
howItWorks.step4: Valid email captured → next DM delivers the lead magnet instantly.
howItWorks.p2: Everything stays inside Instagram. Friction drops, opt-in rates jump.

dataCollectionNode.title: The Data Collection node
dataCollectionNode.p1: Drop the node, set reply type to Email, done. Four features make it solid for email capture:
dataCollectionNode.validationDesc: Only properly formatted addresses are accepted. No typos in your list.
dataCollectionNode.retryDesc: If the user types something else, the node asks again. 3 retries default, configurable.
dataCollectionNode.timeoutDesc: If silence hits, the node waits (1 min - 24 h) then branches to a different DM.
dataCollectionNode.contactFieldDesc: Email lands on the contact's email field, ready for segments, follow-ups, syncs.
dataCollectionNode.p2: Chain more nodes for first name, phone or any custom field.

leadMagnets.title: Lead magnets that convert
leadMagnets.p1: The magnet decides whether the flow works. It has to feel worth an email. Four formats outperform:
leadMagnets.pdfDesc: Short, scannable, niche-specific. "15-point reel hook checklist" beats "complete creator guide."
leadMagnets.templateDesc: Notion, Canva, spreadsheets. High perceived value, low cost to deliver.
leadMagnets.couponDesc: If you sell something, attach a personal code. Doubles as a sales nudge.
leadMagnets.courseDesc: 5-day series on one skill. Keeps open rates high for weeks.

syncAndCompliance.title: Sync & compliance
syncAndCompliance.p1: Captured emails live in your Simpliers CHAT contacts. Export to CSV any time, or push them to your ESP via an External Request node right after Data Collection.
syncAndCompliance.p2: The webhook works with Mailchimp, ConvertKit, ActiveCampaign, Brevo, MailerLite. Set it once, new subscribers land tagged in the right list.
syncAndCompliance.p3: Before asking for an email, make the value exchange explicit ("Drop your email and I'll send the checklist"). For marketing emails, add a second opt-in DM: "Want occasional emails like this? Reply YES."
syncAndCompliance.p4: Always include unsubscribe in your ESP emails. Honor deletion requests from the contacts screen.

qas.question: Which email platforms can I sync with?
qas.answer: Any platform with a webhook or contact-creation API. Mailchimp, ConvertKit, ActiveCampaign, Brevo, MailerLite and Klaviyo all work through External Request.

qas.question: What happens if a follower types a typo or a fake address?
qas.answer: The node validates format on the spot. Invalid input triggers a retry message. You decide how many retries before the flow gives up.

qas.question: Where are the collected emails stored?
qas.answer: On the contact's profile in Simpliers CHAT. Browse, segment or export from Contacts. Nothing leaves your account unless you push to an ESP.

qas.question: Do I need anything extra for GDPR or KVKK?
qas.answer: Make the value exchange explicit, add a second consent message for marketing emails, honor deletion requests from contacts. Same rules as a web signup form.

qas.question: Does this work on the free plan?
qas.answer: Yes. Email collection is on every plan. Paid plans raise the active contact limit and unlock advanced segmentation.

metaApiVsBots.title: Meta API vs Instagram bots: the real difference
metaApiVsBots.description: Meta's Graph API ships DMs through approved endpoints. Browser bots simulate human taps and get accounts disabled. Here's the architecture, the compliance gap, and the red flags before you connect your Instagram.

summary.title: Summary
summary.p1: Two paths automate Instagram. One is built by Meta and ships DMs through official endpoints. The other simulates a human inside a browser. The first carries zero ban risk; the second gets accounts disabled.
summary.k1: Graph API = official endpoints, OAuth login, no password sharing, no ban risk.
summary.k2: Browser bots = headless Chrome or app emulation, password required, high ban risk.
summary.k3: Meta detects bot fingerprints in days, not months.
summary.k4: If a tool cannot show a Graph API integration, treat it as a bot.

coreDifference.title: The core difference
coreDifference.p1: The Graph API is a documented set of endpoints Meta built so approved partners can read comments, reply to DMs and trigger messages on Business and Creator accounts. Calls go server-to-server, signed with an OAuth token you grant through Facebook Login.
coreDifference.p2: Browser bots skip the API entirely. A server somewhere logs into Instagram with your username and password, opens the app, then taps buttons through automation libraries. To Meta, your account looks like a person using two phones in two countries at once.

graphApi.title: How the Graph API works
graphApi.p1: When you connect through a Meta-approved partner, four things happen behind the scenes:
graphApi.endpointsDesc: Reading comments, sending DMs and detecting story replies all use named API calls. No screen scraping, no DOM parsing.
graphApi.oauthDesc: You grant scoped permissions through Facebook Login. The partner gets a token, never your Instagram password.
graphApi.rateLimitDesc: Meta sets call quotas per app. The API rejects bursts that look spammy before they reach your account.
graphApi.complianceDesc: Partners pass a Meta review covering messaging windows, consent and approved triggers. Break the rules and the app loses access, not the user.

browserBots.title: How browser bots work
browserBots.p1: Bots ship as a server task that opens Instagram in a hidden browser or emulator. Four mechanics make them risky:
browserBots.simulateDesc: Headless Chrome, Selenium or Appium drives clicks and types in the web or app interface. Timing, mouse paths and viewport size are mocked.
browserBots.credentialsDesc: No OAuth route exists, so the bot must store your Instagram username and password. Any breach of the vendor leaks the credentials.
browserBots.fingerprintDesc: Same data-center IPs, identical browser canvases, robotic timing. Meta clusters these accounts and disables them in batches.
browserBots.fragileDesc: When Instagram renames a button or shifts a layout, the bot freezes. Vendors patch in days; in the meantime, your automations silently die.
browserBots.p2: The bot can do things the API does not allow, like mass cold DMs or follower scraping. That extra power is exactly what triggers Meta's enforcement.

comparison.title: Side-by-side comparison
comparison.p1: Five dimensions where the two paths diverge:
comparison.archApi: Server-to-server REST calls signed with an OAuth token.
comparison.archBot: Headless browser or emulator that pretends to be a human session.
comparison.complianceApi: Operates inside Meta's published partner policy.
comparison.complianceBot: Violates Meta's terms; every action is technically against the rules.
comparison.rateApi: Quotas enforced by the API; predictable and documented.
comparison.rateBot: No formal limit, but Meta flags volume that looks non-human.
comparison.banApi: Near zero. Approved channel, audit trail, revocable token.
comparison.banBot: High. Shadowbans, restrictions and permanent disables are normal outcomes.
comparison.uptimeApi: Stable; works through password changes, 2FA prompts and device switches.
comparison.uptimeBot: Fragile; breaks when Instagram changes selectors or pushes a 2FA prompt.

redFlags.title: Red flags to watch
redFlags.p1: Marketing pages rarely admit they are bots. Five signals reveal it anyway:
redFlags.r1: Asks for your Instagram username and password during onboarding instead of Facebook Login.
redFlags.r2: Works on Personal accounts — the Graph API only supports Business and Creator.
redFlags.r3: Promises mass cold DMs to followers who never engaged with you.
redFlags.r4: Offers follower scraping, unfollow campaigns or auto-like loops.
redFlags.r5: Is not listed in Meta's Business Partner directory and shows no app review.
redFlags.p2: Any one of these is enough. The right move is to close the tab and pick a Meta-approved partner instead.

qas.question: Is the Meta Graph API really safer or is that just marketing?
qas.answer: Genuinely safer. The API is the channel Meta built and audits. Tools using it cannot break Meta's rules even if they wanted to, because the endpoints reject the calls.

qas.question: Why do bot vendors still exist if they get accounts banned?
qas.answer: Because they sell on growth promises Meta does not allow, like mass DMs and follower scraping. They are cheap to spin up and easy to market. The risk lands on the user, not the vendor.

qas.question: Can I tell from a website whether a tool uses the API or a bot?
qas.answer: Yes. Two checks: does signup go through Facebook Login or an Instagram password field, and does the tool require a Business or Creator account. API tools always do both.

qas.question: What happens if I already gave a bot my password?
qas.answer: Change your Instagram password, revoke the session in Settings, enable 2FA and disconnect the tool. Then switch to an official-API partner before automating again.

qas.question: Does Simpliers CHAT use the Graph API?
qas.answer: Yes. Simpliers CHAT is a Meta Technology Partner and runs entirely on the official Graph API. You connect through Facebook Login; we never see your Instagram password.

dmAutomationRecipes.title: 7 Great Automation Ideas to Save Your DM Inbox
dmAutomationRecipes.description: If you have a growing audience, you know how much of your day goes to answering the same DMs over and over. Bonding with your followers is great, but constantly copy-pasting the same link drains your energy.

toc.anatomy: The anatomy of a good automation

summary.title: Summary
summary.p1: Every creator with a growing audience answers the same DMs over and over. Each one is a recipe: a trigger, a single DM, a clear outcome. Pick one, ship it today, then add the next.
summary.k1: Recipe = trigger keyword or event + DM + outcome.
summary.k2: Seven proven recipes covering links, leads, bookings, sales and events.
summary.k3: Start with the message you type most often. That's the highest-leverage win.
summary.k4: Rewrite the DM in your voice before going live, templates are starting points.
summary.lead: Here are 7 ready-made Flows (automation scenarios) you can set up in just a few minutes with tools like Simpliers CHAT, working in the background in your place. You do not have to set them all up in one day; start with the message you write the most, test it, and add new ones over time.

anatomy.title: The Anatomy of a Good Automation
anatomy.p1: All successful automations consist of three simple steps:
anatomy.triggerDesc: A specific comment, story reply, magic word in a DM, or intent written by the follower is detected.
anatomy.openerDesc: The first reply that goes to your follower. Not robotic; it should be warm and clear, as if it came straight from your own mouth.
anatomy.payloadDesc: Your actual goal. It can be sharing a website link, collecting emails, or sharing a guide-style PDF.
anatomy.p2: Here are 7 proven ideas you can adapt to your own style with this logic:

affiliate.name: Automatically Send Affiliate Links
affiliate.desc: When followers ask about the product in your video, you do not have to send the link one by one.

leadMagnet.name: Collect Emails and Distribute a Free Resource
leadMagnet.desc: If you have a great PDF, template, or guide on hand, use it to build an email list.

booking.name: Consulting and Coaching Appointments
booking.desc: You have openings in your calendar but keep dealing with "What time works for you?" back-and-forth to set appointments; you can send your Google Calendar link.

product.name: Directing Impatient Buyers to the Product
product.desc: Answer "Where can I find this?", "How much is it?" questions instantly, send the product link, and do not miss the sale.

giveaway.name: Automatically Confirm Giveaway Entries
giveaway.desc: It builds trust with your follower, prevents invalid entries by reminding them of the rules, and strengthens your place in the algorithm thanks to the engagement in your DM inbox.

event.name: Live Stream or Event Registrations
event.desc: Just saying "we are live tonight" is not enough. When you put people into a registration system or an email newsletter, you do not leave the attendance rate to chance.

waitlist.name: Pre-Launch Waitlist
waitlist.desc: Build a community ahead of time for a product, course, or service that has not launched yet. Collect details like name, email, and phone in advance, and keep your customer base ready for post-launch sales.

startFirst.title: How Should You Start?
startFirst.p1: Do not overwhelm yourself trying to do everything at once. Move step by step:
startFirst.s1: Find where you lose time: open your DM inbox. Look at which message you replied to manually the most over the last few days. This should be the first Flow you set up.
startFirst.s2: Translate it into your own communication voice: the examples above are just a skeleton. Customize your messages however you like.
startFirst.s3: Start simple: test with a single word on a single post. How do people react? If everything goes well, there is nothing standing in your way to grow your account.

adapt.title: A Few Golden Tips
adapt.keywordTitle: Do Not Make Everyday Words Triggers
adapt.keywordDesc: Do not pick your trigger words from words people frequently use in normal comments like "yes", "great", "nice". Sending an automatic DM to irrelevant comments gets annoying. Be specific: "Guide", "Link", "Price".
adapt.followUpTitle: Do Not Forget to Follow Up
adapt.followUpDesc: This is one of the best parts. If the user did not click the link on the first day, set up a gentle follow-up message the next day through your automation tool, like "Did you have any trouble with the link, can I help you?" It seriously increases conversions.
adapt.measureDesc: Do not set up your Flows and forget them. Are people replying to the message? Are they clicking the link? If there is no return, update your message to be friendlier or make your trigger word more distinct.

qas.question: Should I set up a separate Flow for each different post or idea?
qas.answer: Yes. Each idea runs on its own trigger word.

qas.question: Will this cause Instagram to flag my account as spam or block my links?
qas.answer: If you use trusted tools officially approved by Meta and running over the Graph API (like Simpliers CHAT), you are safe. Instagram already supports businesses and content creators using these kinds of official tools. But if you use unapproved "bot" software that asks for your Instagram password, your account is at risk.

qas.question: How long should my automatic DM text be?
qas.answer: Two or three sentences at most. People do not like reading long paragraphs in their DM inbox, they just skim. In the first message, reply to the comment warmly; in the second message, deliver your content. Be clear and to the point.

qas.question: My following is still small (for example under 1000), is it too early to set up automation?
qas.answer: Absolutely not. If you receive even 3-5 of the same message a day, setting up this system saves you time. Establishing these systems early lets you manage the process without being buried under message piles and without panicking tomorrow when a video of yours goes viral or your audience suddenly grows. Posts with automation set up also have a fairly high chance of landing on Explore.

manualVsAutomated.title: Manual vs. Automated DMs: When Does Answering by Hand Burn Out?
manualVsAutomated.description: There is a point where answering DMs by hand turns into a second job. This guide pins down that point with the numbers, then shows the cleanest way out.

toc.whenSwitch: Signals it is time to switch

summary.title: Summary
summary.p1: Manual DM communication is warm and personal; however, because of the limits of human capacity, the ceiling fills up very fast. A human, only while at the computer or phone and focused, can produce around 20 quality replies an hour on average. Automation, on the other hand, lifts that ceiling completely without breaking your brand's friendly voice.
summary.k1: Manual Capacity Ceiling: At most ~20 quality DMs per hour (only while you are online and focused on a single conversation).
summary.k2: Automation Performance: 24/7 nonstop, in the same second, managing thousands of parallel conversations.
summary.k3: Efficiency Cost: The price of automating the recurring operational load is only ~$15 per month.
summary.k4: Strategic Split: Keep the human touch for closing sales, partnerships and crisis handling; leave the rest to automation.

ceiling.title: The Capacity Ceiling: Where Does Manual Reply Choke?
ceiling.p1: Manual replying has a rigid, hard upper limit. Many creators and brands only notice this limit once it is too late and the DM inbox has exploded. The ceiling of manual communication is squeezed by three big forces:
ceiling.rateTitle: At Most ~20 Quality Replies per Hour
ceiling.rateDesc: Reading a message, understanding what it means, finding and checking the right link, and writing a reply takes time. The realistic human pace for quality messaging that does not brush the follower off and that you can stand behind cannot exceed 20 per hour.
ceiling.offlineDesc: Your followers can DM at midnight, lunchtime or on a weekend holiday. In the manual model, your reply speed depends directly on what you happen to be doing at that very second on the phone (or on whether you are asleep).
ceiling.focusTitle: Only One Conversation at a Time
ceiling.focusDesc: As a human, messaging 10 different people in parallel at the same time makes focus impossible and quickly drops reply quality. After a while you notice that out of fatigue you stop writing long, explanatory messages and start cutting them short.

timeMath.title: The Time Math: The Hidden Cost of Manual Reply
timeMath.p1: Let us assume you are a mid-sized creator or business. Let us calculate the time you spend on the same recurring questions: "What is the price?", "Can I get a link?":
timeMath.p2: In short: every week you spend roughly 10 hours on copy-paste messages. Automation hands those 10 hours back to you for a very small subscription fee.
timeMath.dmsPerDayLabel: DMs needing a reply per day

comparison.title: Side-by-Side Comparison: Manual or Automated?
comparison.p1: Six dimensions where the two approaches diverge:
comparison.speedManual: Takes minutes or hours depending on your online status.
comparison.speedAuto: Within seconds. Delivered the moment the comment or message arrives.
comparison.volumeManual: Capped at ~20 quality replies per hour.
comparison.volumeAuto: Manages thousands of parallel conversations smoothly at the same time.
comparison.hoursManual: Only while you are awake and at the phone.
comparison.hoursAuto: 24/7 nonstop. Launch, holiday or midnight does not matter.
comparison.consistencyManual: Varies by your mood, fatigue and rush.
comparison.consistencyAuto: The same brand language and the same correct link go out every time.
comparison.costManual: Your hourly time, multiplied across the week.
comparison.costAuto: Around $15/month for the typical creator plan.
comparison.dataManual: Whatever you remember, or whatever you can count if you go back.
comparison.dataAuto: Automation runs, link clicks and conversion rates are reported automatically.

whenSwitch.title: "Switch Time" Signals: When Should You Move to Automation?
whenSwitch.p1: If at least two of the 5 signs below match your situation, the manual model is no longer profitable for you:
whenSwitch.s1: You spend more than 1 hour a day on DMs where you keep writing the same things.
whenSwitch.s2: Your reply time goes past 30 minutes and the purchase rates of followers waiting for a link drop.
whenSwitch.s3: You cannot keep up with the flood of messages during giveaway or launch periods.
whenSwitch.s4: You are manually copy-pasting the same link to more than 5 people a day.
whenSwitch.s5: You have started ignoring DMs because the volume in your inbox scares you.

keepManual.title: Hybrid Model: Areas That Should Stay Manual
keepManual.p1: Automation does not have to take over everything. In a smart setup, automation carries 90% of the load, and the remaining strategic 10% is managed manually by you:
keepManual.dealsDesc: Negotiation processes, budget talks and custom scheduling are the heart of your brand. No automation can build the trust relationship here.
keepManual.relationsDesc: Your loyal followers who interact with you constantly, your close friends and your niche circle feel the human touch instantly. Keep the communication with them in your own hands.
keepManual.escalationDesc: Refund requests, criticism or crisis moments. In such cases you need to quickly disable automation and take over the communication calmly and empathetically. With the "Hide Toxic Comments" automation you can manage this process as a hybrid.

qas.question: Will automated DMs feel robotic to my followers?
qas.answer: It comes across however you write it. If you add your own language, brand tone, daily contractions and the emoji you use to your automation, nobody feels like they are talking to a robot. In fact, satisfaction goes up because they get an instant reply.

qas.question: How long does it take to set up the first automation?
qas.answer: With the ready-to-use "Comment to DM" automation as a quick template, setup is only 2 minutes.

qas.question: Does automation reply to every message?
qas.answer: No, and it does not have to. Automate the interactions you choose: post, comment, story reply, incoming DM. You decide which and how.

qas.question: Does automation break that "personal and friendly" feel of the account?
qas.answer: On the contrary, it protects it. By giving you back the hours that go into copy-paste replies, it lets you focus on the deeper conversations that really need a human touch.

qas.question: Can I set up DM automation completely for free?
qas.answer: Yes. Thanks to Simpliers CHAT's free plan, you can build real DM automations without even providing your credit card details, and test your automations live. After seeing with your own eyes how much time it saves your business, as your volume grows you can move to paid plans whenever you want.

howDmAutomationWorks.title: What Is Simpliers CHAT and How Does It Work?
howDmAutomationWorks.description: Simpliers CHAT is a Meta-approved direct message (DM) automation platform built to manage, scale, and turn your Instagram interactions into sales. It organizes messaging processes into a systematic structure for brands, content creators, agencies, and businesses.

toc.journey: How it works behind the scenes

summary.title: Summary
summary.p1: Simpliers CHAT detects the comments, story replies, and direct messages coming to your Instagram account and builds automatic replies within the rules you set.
summary.k1: Use case: nonstop communication around the clock, lead capture (lead generation), and zeroing out manual workload.
summary.k2: Audience: influencers (campaign/link distribution), brands (product sales, customer support), agencies (multi-account management).
summary.k3: No code required: Instagram automation (Flow) is built entirely through a visual interface.
summary.k4: Account requirement: the system only works on Business or Creator accounts.

features.title: Core features of Simpliers CHAT
features.p1: Unlike standard message management, Simpliers CHAT offers features that make the process smarter and more efficient:
features.commentDesc: It detects keywords like info, price, or link written under a post or Reels and instantly sends the user a personalized DM with the relevant details.
features.translateDesc: It automatically translates incoming and outgoing messages. For accounts addressing global audiences, it provides uninterrupted, language-free communication.
features.flowDesc: It lets you build multi-step message scenarios based on user intent (question, complaint, suggestion, request).
features.accountsDesc: It offers the ability to manage multiple Instagram accounts from a single screen for free. It is ideal especially for agencies.
features.dataDesc: It offers the ability to automatically collect users' email, contact details, or request specifics and export them.

journey.title: How it works behind the scenes
journey.p1: Simpliers CHAT runs operations over Meta's official API infrastructure. An automation's workflow consists of these technical steps:

interaction.title: The interaction happens
interaction.desc: The user interacts with your account. This can be a Reels comment, a story reply, or a direct message.

notify.title: Meta sends a notification (webhook layer)
notify.desc: When Meta detects the interaction, it delivers an encrypted data packet (webhook) to Simpliers CHAT servers. Instead of constantly polling for new data, the system receives it from Meta only when an action occurs.

process.title: Simpliers CHAT accessing and processing the data
process.desc: The security and accuracy of the incoming data packet are verified. The text the user wrote is matched against the keywords and rules you set on the platform. Once verified, the relevant Flow (Automation) starts.

prepare.title: The message is prepared
prepare.desc: The message to be sent is combined with variables such as the user's name, special discount code, product link, or PDF file. If active, instant translation is applied and the ready data packet is sent back to the Meta API.

deliver.title: The message is sent
deliver.desc: Meta delivers the prepared reply directly to the follower's DM inbox.

webhook.title: What is Meta's webhook layer?
webhook.p1: The webhook is a messenger shuttling between Instagram and your automation tool. It is a fully event-driven technology that does not force the system to keep polling.
webhook.p2: The three core properties that make it powerful are:
webhook.pushDesc: Meta sends the data the moment the event happens. There is no waiting or constant checking. That is the secret to the speed.
webhook.signedDesc: Every notification is signed with your application's secret key. Automation tools do not act without verifying this signature. This prevents fake requests.
webhook.retryDesc: Even if your automation tool goes briefly offline, Meta does not stop sending the notification and retries at set intervals. The DM is eventually delivered, only a few seconds late.

setup.title: Simpliers CHAT setup steps
setup.p1: Moving from scratch to a live automation system without any coding knowledge is quite simple:
setup.s1: Sign up for Simpliers CHAT and click the "Log In with Instagram" button.
setup.s2: Select the Instagram Business or Creator account you want to connect to the automation.
setup.s3: Approve all requested access permissions in full. (This step is required for the system to read comments and send messages).
setup.s4: Pick a ready-made Flow that fits your needs or build your own automation flow from scratch.
setup.s5: Test your Flow and then activate it.
setup.p2: Setting up your first Flow takes about two minutes. Creating the following scenarios takes less than a minute.

gotchas.title: Common issues and solutions
gotchas.p1: It usually traces back to one of these four reasons:
gotchas.accountDesc: Meta does not grant automation tools access for personal accounts. To use automation, you need to change your account type from your Instagram profile settings.
gotchas.windowDesc: Meta only allows you to send a DM as a reply to an interaction the user started. You cannot send a DM to an account that has never interacted with you.
gotchas.permissionsDesc: The system not replying to comments or DMs is mostly caused by permissions granted incompletely during login. Solution: you need to refresh the permissions from inside the automation tool.
gotchas.permissionsLink: Learn how to refresh your permissions
gotchas.keywordDesc: If you chose the same keyword for two different Flows, only the last one you set up runs. Take care to keep your keywords short, clear, and unique for each Flow.

qas.question: Is Simpliers CHAT safe for my account?
qas.answer: Yes. Simpliers CHAT is an application approved by Meta. All operations are carried out in encrypted form in line with Meta's Graph API rules and security standards. Your account password is never stored on Simpliers servers.

qas.question: Do I need a technical integration or to write code?
qas.answer: No, absolutely not. Connecting the account and building Flows is done entirely through a visual interface. The technical parts are handled automatically in the background.

qas.question: What happens to my messages if Instagram goes down or the connection drops?
qas.answer: When there is an outage in Meta services, Simpliers CHAT queues the notifications in the background. When Instagram systems return to normal, the pending operations are processed in order and the messages are delivered; no data is lost.

qas.question: Can followers tell this message is an automation?
qas.answer: No. The sent DM appears normally in the user's inbox as if it came directly from your account. Whether the communication feels warm and natural depends entirely on the message content you write.

howToTurnCommentsIntoSales.title: How to turn Instagram comments into sales
howToTurnCommentsIntoSales.description: Followers comment with intent — they are the easiest buyers you have. This guide shows how to design the comment-to-sale funnel end to end with Simpliers CHAT.

summary.title: Summary
summary.p1: Follower comments a trigger keyword, automation drops a DM within seconds, they tap to checkout while intent is at peak. Whole game. Works because comments are public skin in the game.
summary.k1: Use a single, ownable CTA keyword (SHOP, COURSE, DEAL) under every promo post and Reel.
summary.k2: Reply in DMs within seconds — speed beats clever copy every time.
summary.k3: Keep the DM short: warm hello, the link, one nudge. No essay.
summary.k4: Filter buyers from browsers with a follow-up question before you send the link.

funnel.title: The comment-to-sale funnel
funnel.step1: Post or Reel with hook + CTA keyword. Keyword is the door.
funnel.step2: Follower comments the keyword. Public micro-commitment, stronger than a like.
funnel.step3: Simpliers CHAT detects it and sends a DM with your product link.
funnel.step4: Follower taps the link without leaving the app and converts.
funnel.p2: Every step measurable: comments, DMs, taps, conversions. Fix the leaking step, not the funnel.

ctas.title: High-intent CTA keywords
ctas.p1: The CTA keyword is the first filter. It sorts curious scrollers from people who actually want what you sell. Pick a keyword that matches the offer and own it across every promo post:
ctas.shopDesc: Drop SHOP under product Reels. Send the product page link plus a discount code in the first DM.
ctas.courseDesc: Use COURSE or the course name. DM the sales page link with one social-proof line above it.
ctas.dealDesc: Time-boxed launches respond well to DEAL or SAVE. Bake the deadline into the DM body so the urgency travels.
ctas.linkDesc: If you sell many products, use LINK + ask one question ("which color?") and route by reply to the right page.

dmSequence.title: Designing the DM sequence
dmSequence.p1: DM is the salesperson. Short, human, skimmable. Four messages cover most cases:
dmSequence.s1Desc: Acknowledge the comment, give the link, one sentence of context. No pitch wall.
dmSequence.s2Desc: One filter question ("bundle or single course?"). Route the next DM by answer.
dmSequence.s3Desc: If no tap, short nudge. One value line + link. Skip if they bought.
dmSequence.s4Desc: On limited offers, last DM hours before deadline. Honest urgency, no fake countdowns.
dmSequence.p2: Write like a friend. No marketing voice, no emoji walls.

optimize.title: Optimizing for revenue
optimize.p1: Once live, optimize one variable at a time:
optimize.o1: Track which posts drive the most keyword comments. Replicate the angle, not the post.
optimize.o2: A/B test the first DM. Same link, two opening lines. Winner becomes the baseline.
optimize.o3: Tag every buyer who came from a keyword. After 30 days you know revenue per comment.
optimize.o4: Add a qualifier for high-ticket. Lower volume, higher close rate — usually a net win.

pitfalls.title: Common pitfalls
pitfalls.p1: Three common causes: keyword too clever, DM too long, link goes to homepage instead of the matching product page. Fix those first.
pitfalls.p2: One keyword per offer, one offer per post. Mix three offers on one Reel and the DM logic — and your buyer — gets confused.

qas.question: What is the best CTA keyword for selling?
qas.answer: A single, short, on-brand word that matches the offer. SHOP for products, COURSE for courses, DEAL for time-boxed promos. Avoid full sentences — short keywords get used more often.

qas.question: How fast should the DM go out after a comment?
qas.answer: Within seconds. Speed is the biggest conversion lever in this funnel. Simpliers CHAT triggers the DM the moment Meta forwards the comment event, so there is no manual delay.

qas.question: Should I send the product link in the very first DM?
qas.answer: For low-ticket items, yes — frictionless wins. For higher-ticket courses or services, ask one qualifying question first and route the link based on the answer.

qas.question: Will Instagram penalize me for selling through DMs?
qas.answer: No. As long as you stay inside Meta's messaging policies and only DM users who triggered the automation themselves, the funnel runs through the official Graph API and is fully compliant.

qas.question: How do I measure if comment-to-sale is actually working?
qas.answer: Track three numbers: comments captured, link taps inside the DM, and tagged conversions on the product page. Simpliers CHAT exposes the first two; UTM parameters on the link give you the third.

qas.question: Is Simpliers CHAT exactly the same as ManyChat? How does it differ?
qas.answer: No. ManyChat is a multi-channel automation platform, whereas Simpliers CHAT is a dedicated DM automation and management solution tailored specifically for Instagram. It offers advanced features not found in ManyChat, such as AI-powered comment moderation and real-time DM translation, combined with a cleaner interface and more affordable pricing.

qas.question: Why focus only on Instagram and not other platforms?
qas.answer: Instead of spreading ourselves too thin across multiple platforms, we chose to go deep into Instagram. Our Instagram-centric architecture allows us to run powerful features like advanced DM automation, comment moderation, AI sentiment analysis, and live DM translation much more smoothly and effectively on a single channel.

qas.question: Can I migrate my existing ManyChat automations to Simpliers CHAT?
qas.answer: Yes. Our support team will guide you through rebuilding your current setups and DM automation workflows on Simpliers CHAT. Most migration and setup processes take less than an hour.

qas.question: Does Simpliers CHAT require my Instagram password? Is it secure?
qas.answer: No. You connect your Instagram account securely via official Facebook/Meta authentication without ever sharing your password. All your DM automation workflows operate in 100% compliance with Meta's official policies.

qas.question: Can I automatically send DMs to users who comment on my posts?
qas.answer: Yes. You can trigger automated DM automation sequences based on specific rules or keywords from Instagram comments, allowing you to fully automate the delivery of links, catalogs, and information.

qas.question: Can I manage incoming DMs and comments from a single dashboard?
qas.answer: Yes. Simpliers CHAT provides a single operational inbox, allowing you to manage your Instagram DM automation workflows and incoming comments efficiently without missing any audience interactions.

qas.question: How does AI comment moderation work? Can I hide spam and toxic comments?
qas.answer: Yes. Our advanced AI scans every incoming comment and scores its sentiment. It automatically hides toxic remarks, spam, or comments that could harm your brand image, keeping your DM automation and comment sections clean and professional. You always retain full control — you can review and unhide any comment whenever you want.

qas.question: How can I manage messages received in different languages?
qas.answer: With Simpliers CHAT's integrated DM translation feature, you can translate incoming and outgoing messages in real time across more than 20 languages. This allows you to seamlessly run your DM automation and live support without needing to copy-paste or switch apps.

qas.question: Can I use Simpliers CHAT with my team?
qas.answer: Yes. Beyond DM automation, you can assign conversations and comments that require manual handling to specific team members, tracking customer messages in an organized, synchronized manner with multi-user support.

qas.question: Can I still manually reply to DMs while automation is active?
qas.answer: Yes, you are in control at every step. DM automation workflows are designed to provide lightning-fast responses on Instagram while you focus on content creation or your business. You can jump into the live chat and take over the automation at any moment.

qas.question: Is Simpliers CHAT more affordable than ManyChat?
qas.answer: Yes, it offers a more budget-friendly option across all plan comparisons. You get access to advanced DM automation features at much more competitive rates. You can find all the details and choose the best plan for you on our pricing table.

qas.question: Can I get a corporate invoice for my business?
qas.answer: Yes. Valid legal corporate invoices are automatically generated for all your subscription purchases to meet your company's accounting requirements.

qas.question: Do you provide setup support and onboarding assistance?
qas.answer: Yes. Simpliers CHAT users receive comprehensive onboarding assistance and 1-on-1 live consultation from our expert team during the initial DM automation setup and system integration process.
